item.title: Long Beach pulls out of after-school program it helped run for decades; over 80 city employees at risk of layoffs
item.description:

The post Long Beach pulls out of after-school program it helped run for decades; over 80 city employees at risk of layoffs appeared first on Long Beach Post News.
item.source:markdown:
![Parents walk their kids to Edison Elementary School on the first day of school in Long Beach August 29, 2018. Photo by Thomas R Cordova/Safe Streets](https://img.lbpost.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/09/10144432/0829-Stuff-256.jpg)
State funding for the program is intact, and the school district is looking for an operator, but the city won’t participate, hoping to save money from its own budget.
The post [Long Beach pulls out of after-school program it helped run for decades; over 80 city employees at risk of layoffs](https://lbpost.com/news/education/long-beach-wrap-after-school-program-layoffs) appeared first on [Long Beach Post News](https://lbpost.com/news).

item.title: Most California senators supported this ADA lawsuit bill. It died anyway
item.description:
Sen. Roger Niello’s proposal to give businesses more time to fix ADA violations before facing lawsuits won overwhelming Senate support. But it died without an Assembly hearing after judiciary chair Ash Kalra rejected the bill in favor of a competing approach.

The post Most California senators supported this ADA lawsuit bill. It died anyway appeared first on Long Beach Post News.
item.source:markdown:

Sen. Roger Niello’s proposal to give businesses more time to fix ADA violations before facing lawsuits won overwhelming Senate support. But it died without an Assembly hearing after judiciary chair Ash Kalra rejected the bill in favor of a competing approach.

item.title: Long Beach hopes to fix parking downtown. It says there are 2 garages that rarely fill up.
item.description:
Ask a city expert and they’ll make the case: It’s not a lack of parking that ails downtown Long Beach. It’s a lack of drivers willing to pay for it. 

The post Long Beach hopes to fix parking downtown. It says there are 2 garages that rarely fill up. appeared first on Long Beach Post News.

item.title: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, rejecting Trump’s proposed limits
item.description:
Trump’s order would have upended widely held views that the 14th Amendment confers citizenship on everyone born in the U.S., excluding only the children of foreign diplomats and those born to a foreign occupying force.

The post Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, rejecting Trump’s proposed limits appeared first on Long Beach Post News.

item.title: LA County drug overdose deaths declined for third straight year in 2025, mirroring national trend
item.description:

item.title: Trump renews push to shift homelessness funding. What’s at stake
item.description:
The Trump administration wants to shift more money to homeless shelters that require sobriety, a change that would disrupt California’s “housing-first” policies.

The post Trump renews push to shift homelessness funding. What’s at stake appeared first on Long Beach Post News.

The Trump administration wants to shift more money to homeless shelters that require sobriety, a change that would disrupt California’s “housing-first” policies.
The post [Trump renews push to shift homelessness funding. What’s at stake](https://lbpost.com/news/trump-renews-push-to-shift-homelessness-funding-whats-at-stake) appeared first on [Long Beach Post News](https://lbpost.com/news).

item.title: Harbor Commission OKs $1.05B budget for Port with increased capital improvement spending
item.description:
Port officials noted the increase in spending reflects a 53.7% increase in capital expenditures compared to the prior year, to $571.8 million, as work continues on delivering the Pier B On-Dock Rail Support Facility.

The post Harbor Commission OKs $1.05B budget for Port with increased capital improvement spending appeared first on Long Beach Post News.
item.source:markdown:
![](https://img.lbpost.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/14120959/0929-Helicopter-839.jpg)
Port officials noted the increase in spending reflects a 53.7% increase in capital expenditures compared to the prior year, to $571.8 million, as work continues on delivering the Pier B On-Dock Rail Support Facility.
The post [Harbor Commission OKs $1.05B budget for Port with increased capital improvement spending](https://lbpost.com/news/harbor-commission-oks-1-05b-budget-for-port-with-increased-capital-improvement-spending) appeared first on [Long Beach Post News](https://lbpost.com/news).

item.title: Ex-parks superintendent charged with secretly recording naked lifeguards in Orange County
item.description:
Kevin Pearsall, who lives in Long Beach, is accused of secretly filming 23 people in the men’s locker room at Bolsa Chica State Beach Lifeguard Headquarters.

The post Ex-parks superintendent charged with secretly recording naked lifeguards in Orange County appeared first on Long Beach Post News.

item.title: Long Beach Pride faces debt crisis, potential building sale after festival collapse
item.description:
The nonprofit running the annual festival was already in debt, according to its outgoing president, and it now must find a way to pay back ticketholders, vendors and sponsors.

The post Long Beach Pride faces debt crisis, potential building sale after festival collapse appeared first on Long Beach Post News.

The nonprofit running the annual festival was already in debt, according to its outgoing president, and it now must find a way to pay back ticketholders, vendors and sponsors.

item.title: New earthquake study finds San Andreas fault is primed for a big quake
item.description:
Scientists found that the San Andreas and San Jacinto faults are under more stress than at any point in the last 1,000 years, meaning that a massive earthquake could be on the way.

The post New earthquake study finds San Andreas fault is primed for a big quake appeared first on Long Beach Post News.
item.source:markdown:
![](https://img.lbpost.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/10/02143032/0929-Helicopter-3.jpg)
Scientists found that the San Andreas and San Jacinto faults are under more stress than at any point in the last 1,000 years, meaning that a massive earthquake could be on the way.
The post [New earthquake study finds San Andreas fault is primed for a big quake](https://lbpost.com/news/new-earthquake-study-finds-san-andreas-fault-is-primed-for-a-big-quake) appeared first on [Long Beach Post News](https://lbpost.com/news).

item.title: Where to watch fireworks in and around Long Beach this 4th of July
item.description:
There's a supersized fireworks show planned all along the city's coastline on July 4, a June 26 celebration at the Port of LA and a July 3 show in Los Alamitos.

The post Where to watch fireworks in and around Long Beach this 4th of July appeared first on Long Beach Post News.
item.source:markdown:
![](https://img.lbpost.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/10161629/070518-QM4th-198-TRC.jpg)

The post [Where to watch fireworks in and around Long Beach this 4th of July](https://lbpost.com/news/where-to-watch-fireworks-long-beach-4th-of-july-250th) appeared first on [Long Beach Post News](https://lbpost.com/news).
